title: SpectralAdvisory
description: Schema for a single spectral advisory entry persisted on an actor profile under learning_state.spectral_advisories. Produced by the rhythm_fft_analysis daemon task in lumina.signals.advisories.upsert_spectral_advisory and consumed by per-domain advisory consumers (education journal_adapters, future agriculture/equipment dashboards). The entry is keyed for dedup purposes by the (signal, band) tuple; a new finding for the same key replaces any existing entry.

advisory_id.description: RFC-4122 UUID assigned at upsert time. Stable for the lifetime of the entry; consumers may use it as a deduplication key when surfacing an advisory across multiple UI refreshes.

signal.description: Name of the signal that produced this advisory (e.g. 'valence', 'soil_pH', 'motor_vibration'). Must match a signal name declared in the producing domain's domain-physics.json signals block.

band.description: Spectral band whose drift triggered the advisory (e.g. 'dc_drift', 'circaseptan', 'ultradian', 'intra_hour'). Band names are domain-defined via the per-signal bands map; the framework supplies defaults but does not constrain the keyset.

direction.description: Sign of the spectral drift relative to the historical band signature. 'positive' = signal trending up against its baseline at this period; 'negative' = trending down; 'neutral' = magnitude-only finding with no signed direction; '*' reserved for wildcard cases.

z_score.description: Magnitude of the drift expressed as a z-score against the per-band variance window. The producing domain's spectral_drift_thresholds.k_spectral controls the trigger threshold.

message.description: Human-readable advisory message rendered at upsert time via lumina.signals.templates.render_advisory_message. Domain physics may inject per-signal message_overrides; otherwise the framework default template is used. Consumers should treat this string as opaque.

created_utc.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp at which the advisory was created or most recently re-upserted. Used for tie-breaking when multiple advisories share the same priority class.

expires_utc.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp after which the advisory is considered stale and should be pruned from the persisted list and ignored by consumers. Computed as created_utc + advisory_ttl_seconds (per-signal optional override, framework default 86400).
